item.title: Magnetic structure of ${\mathrm{EuZn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2}$ single-crystal thin film
item.description:
Author(s): Yu Wei Soh, Hsiang Lee, Eugen Weschke, Shinichi Nishihaya, Mikhael T. Sayat, Masaki Uchida, and Jian-Rui Soh

Magnetic topological materials are a class of compounds which can host massless electrons controlled by the magnetic order. One such compound is ${\mathrm{EuZn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2}$, which has recently garnered interest due to its strong interplay between the Eu magnetism and charge carriers. Howe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054425] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu Wei Soh, Hsiang Lee, Eugen Weschke, Shinichi Nishihaya, Mikhael T. Sayat, Masaki Uchida, and Jian-Rui Soh

Magnetic topological materials are a class of compounds which can host massless electrons controlled by the magnetic order. One such compound is EuZn2Sb2, which has recently garnered interest due to its strong interplay between the Eu magnetism and charge carriers. However, the topology of the elect…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054425] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic structure of ${\mathrm{EuZn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2}$ single-crystal thin film
item.dc:creator: Yu Wei Soh, Hsiang Lee, Eugen Weschke, Shinichi Nishihaya, Mikhael T. Sayat, Masaki Uchida, and Jian-Rui Soh

item.title: Spin-orbit and topological contributions to magnetic anisotropy in monolayer ${\text{V}}_{2}\text{W}{\text{S}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Bing Wang, Yihang Bai, Changgeng Li, Shuyuan Liu, Fengzhu Ren, Chongze Wang, and Jun-Hyung Cho

Two-dimensional magnetic topological insulators, such as monolayer ${\text{V}}_{2}\text{W}{\text{S}}_{4}$, are promising platforms for realizing the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) effect, with high Curie temperatures and large perpendicular magnetic anisotropy energies (MAEs). Using first-principles d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064419] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bing Wang, Yihang Bai, Changgeng Li, Shuyuan Liu, Fengzhu Ren, Chongze Wang, and Jun-Hyung Cho

Two-dimensional magnetic topological insulators, such as monolayer V2WS4, are promising platforms for realizing the quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) effect, with high Curie temperatures and large perpendicular magnetic anisotropy energies (MAEs). Using first-principles density functional theory calculat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064419] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit and topological contributions to magnetic anisotropy in monolayer ${\text{V}}_{2}\text{W}{\text{S}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: Bing Wang, Yihang Bai, Changgeng Li, Shuyuan Liu, Fengzhu Ren, Chongze Wang, and Jun-Hyung Cho

item.title: Shuttling Majorana zero modes in disordered and noisy topological superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Bill P. Truong, Kartiek Agarwal, and T. Pereg-Barnea

The braiding of Majorana zero modes (MZMs) forms the fundamental building block for topological quantum computation. Braiding protocols which involve the physical exchange of MZMs are typically envisioned on a network of topological superconducting wires. An essential component of these protocols is…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064506] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bill P. Truong, Kartiek Agarwal, and T. Pereg-Barnea

The braiding of Majorana zero modes (MZMs) forms the fundamental building block for topological quantum computation. Braiding protocols which involve the physical exchange of MZMs are typically envisioned on a network of topological superconducting wires. An essential component of these protocols is…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064506] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Shuttling Majorana zero modes in disordered and noisy topological superconductors
item.dc:creator: Bill P. Truong, Kartiek Agarwal, and T. Pereg-Barnea

item.title: Self-consistent renormalized spin-wave theory of magnetic and topological transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb ferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Jian-Lin Li and Chien-Te Wu

We investigate finite-temperature magnetic and topological phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb ferromagnets using an extended self-consistent renormalized spin-wave theory (SRSWT) that incorporates higher-order corrections from the Holstein-Primakoff expansion. Focusing on the combined ef…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075129] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian-Lin Li and Chien-Te Wu

We investigate finite-temperature magnetic and topological phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb ferromagnets using an extended self-consistent renormalized spin-wave theory (SRSWT) that incorporates higher-order corrections from the Holstein-Primakoff expansion. Focusing on the combined ef…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075129] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Self-consistent renormalized spin-wave theory of magnetic and topological transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb ferromagnets

item.title: Single-gate tracking behavior in flat-band multilayer graphene devices
item.description:
Author(s): Kryštof Kolář, Dacen Waters, Joshua Folk, Matthew Yankowitz, and Cyprian Lewandowski

A central feature of many van der Waals (vdW) materials is the ability to precisely control their charge doping, $n$, and electric displacement field, $D$, using top and bottom gates. For devices composed of only a few layers, it is commonly assumed that $D$ causes the layer-by-layer potential to dr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075131] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kryštof Kolář, Dacen Waters, Joshua Folk, Matthew Yankowitz, and Cyprian Lewandowski

A central feature of many van der Waals (vdW) materials is the ability to precisely control their charge doping, n, and electric displacement field, D, using top and bottom gates. For devices composed of only a few layers, it is commonly assumed that D causes the layer-by-layer potential to drop lin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075131] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-gate tracking behavior in flat-band multilayer graphene devices

item.title: Magnetization-induced reordering of ground states phase diagram in a two-component Bose-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Oskar Stachowiak, Hubert Dunikowski, and Emilia Witkowska

We investigate the influence of nonzero magnetization on the ground state phase diagram of the two-component Bose-Hubbard model. Employing a mean-field theoretical framework both analytically and numerically, we demonstrate that the positions and sizes of specific phases on the diagram are magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075132] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Oskar Stachowiak, Hubert Dunikowski, and Emilia Witkowska

We investigate the influence of nonzero magnetization on the ground state phase diagram of the two-component Bose-Hubbard model. Employing a mean-field theoretical framework both analytically and numerically, we demonstrate that the positions and sizes of specific phases on the diagram are magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075132] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026

item.title: Photonic spin Hall effect dependent on Landau level transitions in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Qiaoyun Ma, Hui Dou, Yiting Chen, Guangyi Jia, and Xinxing Zhou

Landau level (LL) engineered photonic spin Hall effect (PSHE) holds great promise for nanoscale manipulation and steering of magneto-optical transport in two-dimensional atomic systems. Herein, we theoretically investigate PSHE modulated by LL transitions $δn={n}^{′}−n=0, ±2$ (where $n$ and n' indic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075420] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qiaoyun Ma, Hui Dou, Yiting Chen, Guangyi Jia, and Xinxing Zhou

Landau level (LL) engineered photonic spin Hall effect (PSHE) holds great promise for nanoscale manipulation and steering of magneto-optical transport in two-dimensional atomic systems. Herein, we theoretically investigate PSHE modulated by LL transitions δn=n′−n=0, ±2 (where n and n' indicate the L…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075420] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Photonic spin Hall effect dependent on Landau level transitions in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$

item.title: Tailored massive Dirac fermions: Directional flat bands and hyperbolic optical response
item.description:
Author(s): Yueheng Du, Lei Sun, Haotian Sun, Chao Ding, and Mingwen Zhao

The low-energy carriers governed by a massive Dirac Hamiltonian in two-dimensional materials offer exciting opportunities for electronic and optoelectronic applications, but they are typically isotropic in nature. In this study, we explore massive Dirac fermions in monolayer ${\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075421] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yueheng Du, Lei Sun, Haotian Sun, Chao Ding, and Mingwen Zhao

The low-energy carriers governed by a massive Dirac Hamiltonian in two-dimensional materials offer exciting opportunities for electronic and optoelectronic applications, but they are typically isotropic in nature. In this study, we explore massive Dirac fermions in monolayer MoS2 subject to a one-di…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075421] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Tailored massive Dirac fermions: Directional flat bands and hyperbolic optical response
item.dc:creator: Yueheng Du, Lei Sun, Haotian Sun, Chao Ding, and Mingwen Zhao

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Distinguishing Majorana bound states from accidental zero-energy modes with a microwave cavity
item.description:
Author(s): Sarath Prem, Olesia Dmytruk, and Mircea Trif

Here, the authors show that the nonlocal nature of Majorana bound states is directly encoded in their microwave response. Introducing the visibility of microwave absorption—a parity-dependent quantity analogous to optical interference contrast—the work reveals that Majorana states exhibit a nonzero visibility only when the cavity couples simultaneously to both wire ends, in stark contrast to trivial zero-energy modes. This nonlocal criterion is robust against disorder and tunnel barriers, and is directly accessible in current experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085420] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sarath Prem, Olesia Dmytruk, and Mircea Trif

Here, the authors show that the nonlocal nature of Majorana bound states is directly encoded in their microwave response. Introducing the visibility of microwave absorption—a parity-dependent quantity analogous to optical interference contrast—the work reveals that Majorana states exhibit a nonzero visibility only when the cavity couples simultaneously to both wire ends, in stark contrast to trivial zero-energy modes. This nonlocal criterion is robust against disorder and tunnel barriers, and is directly accessible in current experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085420] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Distinguishing Majorana bound states from accidental zero-energy modes with a microwave cavity
item.dc:creator: Sarath Prem, Olesia Dmytruk, and Mircea Trif
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Site-polarized Mott phases competing with a correlated metal in twisted ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Gautam Rai, Ammon Fischer, Valentin Crépel, Lede Xian, Angel Rubio, Dante M. Kennes, Roser Valentí, Andrew J. Millis, Antoine Georges, and Tim O. Wehling

Twisted ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ hosts superconductivity, metal-insulator phase transitions, and field-controllable Fermi-liquid to non-Fermi-liquid transport properties. In this work, we use dynamical mean-field theory to provide a coherent understanding of the electronic correlations shaping the twist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L081106] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Gautam Rai, Ammon Fischer, Valentin Crépel, Lede Xian, Angel Rubio, Dante M. Kennes, Roser Valentí, Andrew J. Millis, Antoine Georges, and Tim O. Wehling

Twisted WSe2 hosts superconductivity, metal-insulator phase transitions, and field-controllable Fermi-liquid to non-Fermi-liquid transport properties. In this work, we use dynamical mean-field theory to provide a coherent understanding of the electronic correlations shaping the twisted WSe2 phase di…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L081106] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026

item.title: Quantum fluctuations associated with a first-order magnetic transition in the frustrated kagome lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{d}}_{3}\mathrm{ScB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zhongchen Xu, Xinyang Liu, Cuiwei Zhang, Shuai Zhang, Feng Jin, Junsen Xiang, Quansheng Wu, Xianmin Zhang, Peijie Sun, and Youguo Shi

Intense quantum fluctuations arising from geometrical frustrations in kagome lattice magnets provide a feasible approach to exotic quantum states. Here, we document an unexpected isosymmetric first-order magnetic transition in the recently synthesized frustrated kagome lattice antiferromagnet $\math…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054421] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026

Author(s): Zhongchen Xu, Xinyang Liu, Cuiwei Zhang, Shuai Zhang, Feng Jin, Junsen Xiang, Quansheng Wu, Xianmin Zhang, Peijie Sun, and Youguo Shi

Intense quantum fluctuations arising from geometrical frustrations in kagome lattice magnets provide a feasible approach to exotic quantum states. Here, we document an unexpected isosymmetric first-order magnetic transition in the recently synthesized frustrated kagome lattice antiferromagnet Nd3ScB…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054421] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum fluctuations associated with a first-order magnetic transition in the frustrated kagome lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{d}}_{3}\mathrm{ScB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Extended strange metal regime from superconducting puddles
item.description:
Author(s): Noga Bashan, Evyatar Tulipman, Steven A. Kivelson, Jörg Schmalian, and Erez Berg

Here, the authors show that quantum-fluctuating superconducting puddles embedded in a metallic matrix act as dynamical impurities whose inelastic Andreev processes generate strange-metal transport over an extended regime of microscopic parameters. The resulting marginal Fermi liquid window is parametrically wide and continues down to exponentially small temperatures set by a charge-Kondo scale, producing T-linear resistivity and Tln(1/T) corrections to specific heat and thermopower. This mechanism is of relevance to disordered unconventional superconductors with a short coherence length, including cuprate high-Tc materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075124] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Noga Bashan, Evyatar Tulipman, Steven A. Kivelson, Jörg Schmalian, and Erez Berg

Here, the authors show that quantum-fluctuating superconducting puddles embedded in a metallic matrix act as dynamical impurities whose inelastic Andreev processes generate strange-metal transport over an extended regime of microscopic parameters. The resulting marginal Fermi liquid window is parametrically wide and continues down to exponentially small temperatures set by a charge-Kondo scale, producing T-linear resistivity and Tln(1/T) corrections to specific heat and thermopower. This mechanism is of relevance to disordered unconventional superconductors with a short coherence length, including cuprate high-Tc materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075124] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Extended strange metal regime from superconducting puddles
item.dc:creator: Noga Bashan, Evyatar Tulipman, Steven A. Kivelson, Jörg Schmalian, and Erez Berg

item.title: Generic Mott transition in the sine-Gordon model through an embedded worm algorithm
item.description:
Author(s): Oscar Bouverot-Dupuis, Laura Foini, and Alberto Rosso

The generic Mott transition in one-dimensional quantum systems can be described by the $(1+1)D$ sine-Gordon model with a tilt via bosonization. Because the configuration space of the sine-Gordon model separates into distinct topological sectors, standard local Monte Carlo schemes are limited to very…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075126] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026

The generic Mott transition in one-dimensional quantum systems can be described by the (1+1)D sine-Gordon model with a tilt via bosonization. Because the configuration space of the sine-Gordon model separates into distinct topological sectors, standard local Monte Carlo schemes are limited to very s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075126] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026

item.title: Local expression of fractional corner charges in obstructed atomic insulators and relationship with the fractional disclination charges
item.description:
Author(s): Hidetoshi Wada and Shuichi Murakami

In obstructed atomic insulators, fractionally quantized charges appear at the corners of the crystals in the shapes of vertex-transitive polyhedra and are given by the filling anomaly divided by the number of corners. Authors of recent studies have revealed that the filling anomaly for the cases wit…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075306] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hidetoshi Wada and Shuichi Murakami

In obstructed atomic insulators, fractionally quantized charges appear at the corners of the crystals in the shapes of vertex-transitive polyhedra and are given by the filling anomaly divided by the number of corners. Authors of recent studies have revealed that the filling anomaly for the cases wit…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075306] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Local expression of fractional corner charges in obstructed atomic insulators and relationship with the fractional disclination charges

item.dc:subject: Semiconductors II: surfaces, interfaces, microstructures, and related topics
item.prism:section: Semiconductors II: surfaces, interfaces, microstructures, and related topics

item.title: Topological phase diagram of twisted bilayer graphene as a function of the twist angle
item.description:
Author(s): Leonardo A. Navarro-Labastida, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Gerardo G. Naumis

Twisted bilayer graphene hosts multiple topological phase transitions between magic angles, driven by hybridization between flat and remote bands. By tracking the evolution of charge distribution, Chern number, quantum metric, and orbital magnetic energy as a function of twist angle, the authors uncover here previously unreported phases with Chern number C=±2. These results reveal how band inversions at high-symmetry points control topology in moiré materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085124] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Leonardo A. Navarro-Labastida, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Gerardo G. Naumis

Twisted bilayer graphene hosts multiple topological phase transitions between magic angles, driven by hybridization between flat and remote bands. By tracking the evolution of charge distribution, Chern number, quantum metric, and orbital magnetic energy as a function of twist angle, the authors uncover here previously unreported phases with Chern number C=±2. These results reveal how band inversions at high-symmetry points control topology in moiré materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085124] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological phase diagram of twisted bilayer graphene as a function of the twist angle
item.dc:creator: Leonardo A. Navarro-Labastida, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Gerardo G. Naumis

item.title: Quantum Hall Andreev conversion in graphene nanostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Alexey Bondarev, William H. Klein, and Harold U. Baranger

We study Andreev conversion in clean nanostructures containing an interface between graphene in the quantum Hall (QH) state and a superconductor, focusing on the lowest Landau level. First, several graphene nanostructures formed from zigzag edges with sharp corners are considered using a tight-bindi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085417] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026

item.title: Determining adsorbate diffusion coefficients on surfaces from helium-3 spin-echo measurements of atomistic dynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Min Lin (林旻), Sam M. Lambrick, and Andrew P. Jardine

Surface diffusion is typically described using tracer diffusion coefficients ${D}_{s}$, which provides a bridge between atomistic dynamics and macroscopic modeling. Here we introduce a new framework to convert helium-3 spin-echo (HeSE) coherent scattering information into tracer diffusion coefficien…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085418] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Min Lin (林旻), Sam M. Lambrick, and Andrew P. Jardine

Surface diffusion is typically described using tracer diffusion coefficients Ds, which provides a bridge between atomistic dynamics and macroscopic modeling. Here we introduce a new framework to convert helium-3 spin-echo (HeSE) coherent scattering information into tracer diffusion coefficients Ds, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085418] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026

item.title: Controllable folding of graphene induced by localized tension
item.description:
Author(s): Pan Shi, Yao Chen, Jingyi Zhang, Jian Feng, and Jie Yang

Graphene is prone to out-of-plane instabilities, with buckling under compression being the most common example. While tensile loading is generally expected to suppress such effects, recent studies on macroscopic elastic sheets have shown that localized tension can induce transverse buckling and gian…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054109] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pan Shi, Yao Chen, Jingyi Zhang, Jian Feng, and Jie Yang

Graphene is prone to out-of-plane instabilities, with buckling under compression being the most common example. While tensile loading is generally expected to suppress such effects, recent studies on macroscopic elastic sheets have shown that localized tension can induce transverse buckling and gian…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054109] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026

item.title: Optical control of skyrmion trajectories via skyrmion number currents
item.description:
Author(s): Emir Syahreza Fadhilla, M. Shoufie Ukhtary, Ardian Nata Atmaja, and Bobby Eka Gunara

We propose a mechanism to control magnetic skyrmion motion by generating a skyrmion number current. This current is produced and controlled via an explicitly time-dependent Hamiltonian containing a Zeeman term arising from the interaction between the spin system and circularly polarized light. We fi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054418] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026

This current is produced and controlled via an explicitly time-dependent Hamiltonian containing a Zeeman term arising from the interaction between the spin system and circularly polarized light. We fi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054418] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026

item.title: Scaling behavior of dissipative systems with imaginary gap closing
item.description:
Author(s): Jinghui Pi, Xingli Li, and Yangqian Yan

Point-gap topology, characterized by spectral winding numbers, is crucial to non-Hermitian topological phases and dramatically alters real-time dynamics. In this paper, we study the evolution of quantum particles in dissipative systems with imaginary gap closing, using the saddle-point approximation…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064302] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:

In this paper, we study the evolution of quantum particles in dissipative systems with imaginary gap closing, using the saddle-point approximation…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064302] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026

item.title: Shot noise in strongly correlated double quantum spin Hall edges
item.description:
Author(s): Andreas Tsantilas, Trithep Devakul, and Julian May-Mann

We consider the effects of interactions on the edges of “double” quantum spin Hall insulators (DQSHIs), motivated by recent experiments on moiré twisted metal dichalcogenides. Without interactions, a DQSHI can be understood as two copies of a conventional quantum spin Hall insulator. If interactions…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075125] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Andreas Tsantilas, Trithep Devakul, and Julian May-Mann

We consider the effects of interactions on the edges of “double” quantum spin Hall insulators (DQSHIs), motivated by recent experiments on moiré twisted metal dichalcogenides. Without interactions, a DQSHI can be understood as two copies of a conventional quantum spin Hall insulator. If interactions…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075125] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Shot noise in strongly correlated double quantum spin Hall edges
item.dc:creator: Andreas Tsantilas, Trithep Devakul, and Julian May-Mann

item.title: Non-Hermitian skin effect and Majorana signature in a Kitaev chain with unidirectional hopping disorder
item.description:
Author(s): Jia-Rui Li, Cui Jiang, Kai Du, Lian-Lian Zhang, and Wei-Jiang Gong

We propose a one-dimensional non-Hermitian Kitaev chain with unidirectional hopping disorder. Through theoretical analysis and numerical calculations, we find that as disorder increases, this system exhibits rich transition phenomena, including skin effect transitions and transitions of Majorana zer…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075419] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026

Through theoretical analysis and numerical calculations, we find that as disorder increases, this system exhibits rich transition phenomena, including skin effect transitions and transitions of Majorana zer…

item.title: Efficient terahertz sensing with laser-induced undetected photons
item.description:
Author(s): S. E. Shafraniuk

An obstacle to utilizing the entangled photons nowadays is the low efficiency of their creation. As a solution, we consider the generation of coherent photons using coupled graphene quantum dot (GQD) lasers activated with a short voltage pulse. To illustrate the method, we analyze the terahertz (THz…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085416] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. E. Shafraniuk

An obstacle to utilizing the entangled photons nowadays is the low efficiency of their creation. As a solution, we consider the generation of coherent photons using coupled graphene quantum dot (GQD) lasers activated with a short voltage pulse. To illustrate the method, we analyze the terahertz (THz…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085416] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Efficient terahertz sensing with laser-induced undetected photons

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Ferromagnetic fragmented state in the pyrochlore ${\mathrm{Ho}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ru}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.description:
Author(s): F. Museur, J. Robert, F. Morineau, N. Bujault, V. Simonet, E. Pachoud, A. Hadj-Azzem, C. V. Colin, L. Mangin-Thro, P. Manuel, J. R. Stewart, P. C. W. Holdsworth, and E. Lhotel

Here, the authors reveal how two distinct sublattices interact to produce an unconventional magnetic phase in the pyrochlore Ho2Ru2O7. Using neutron scattering along side thermodynamic probes, they show that Ru moments first establish an easy-plane antiferromagnetic state, which then mediates an unusual low-temperature ferromagnetic ordering of Ho moments. This work reveals two consecutive, symmetry breaking phase transitions and identifies a fragmented ferromagnetic state analogous to kagome ice. Thus, it highlights the subtle interplay between exchange, anisotropy, and frustration on interpenetrating lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060406] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Museur, J. Robert, F. Morineau, N. Bujault, V. Simonet, E. Pachoud, A. Hadj-Azzem, C. V. Colin, L. Mangin-Thro, P. Manuel, J. R. Stewart, P. C. W. Holdsworth, and E. Lhotel

Here, the authors reveal how two distinct sublattices interact to produce an unconventional magnetic phase in the pyrochlore Ho2Ru2O7. Using neutron scattering along side thermodynamic probes, they show that Ru moments first establish an easy-plane antiferromagnetic state, which then mediates an unusual low-temperature ferromagnetic ordering of Ho moments. This work reveals two consecutive, symmetry breaking phase transitions and identifies a fragmented ferromagnetic state analogous to kagome ice. Thus, it highlights the subtle interplay between exchange, anisotropy, and frustration on interpenetrating lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060406] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferromagnetic fragmented state in the pyrochlore ${\mathrm{Ho}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ru}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.dc:creator: F. Museur, J. Robert, F. Morineau, N. Bujault, V. Simonet, E. Pachoud, A. Hadj-Azzem, C. V. Colin, L. Mangin-Thro, P. Manuel, J. R. Stewart, P. C. W. Holdsworth, and E. Lhotel

item.title: Emergent boundary supersymmetry in a one-dimensional superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Parameshwar R. Pasnoori, Patrick Azaria, Colin Rylands, and Natan Andrei

The interplay between bulk properties and boundary conditions in one-dimensional quantum systems gives rise to many intriguing phenomena. These include the emergence of zero-energy modes which are of significant interest to a variety of fields. In this work we investigate the presence of such zero m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054509] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

These include the emergence of zero-energy modes which are of significant interest to a variety of fields. In this work we investigate the presence of such zero m…

item.title: Spin-orbital intertwined topological superconductivity in a class of correlated noncentrosymmetric materials
item.description:
Author(s): Lichuan Wang, Ran Wang, Xinliang Huang, Xianxin Wu, and Ning Hao

In this study, we propose an alternative route to realizing topological superconductivity (TSC). Our approach targets a new class of correlated noncentrosymmetric materials that host two spin-split Fermi surfaces due to spin-orbital intertwined effects. By investigating the superconducting pairings …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064505] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

Our approach targets a new class of correlated noncentrosymmetric materials that host two spin-split Fermi surfaces due to spin-orbital intertwined effects. By investigating the superconducting pairings …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064505] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

item.title: Interplay between symmetry breaking and interactions in a symmetry protected topological phase
item.description:
Author(s): Parameshwar R. Pasnoori and Patrick Azaria

We solve the one-dimensional massive Thirring model or, equivalently, the sine-Gordon model in the repulsive regime with general Dirichlet boundary conditions, which are characterized by two boundary fields ${ϕ}_{L,R}$ associated with the left and right boundaries, respectively. In the presence of t…

We solve the one-dimensional massive Thirring model or, equivalently, the sine-Gordon model in the repulsive regime with general Dirichlet boundary conditions, which are characterized by two boundary fields ϕL,R associated with the left and right boundaries, respectively. In the presence of these bo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075121] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

item.title: Mott magnetism, structural distortion, and superconductivity induced by interstitial electrons in calcium iodine electrides
item.description:
Author(s): Chi Ding, Zhongwei Zhang, Kairui Zhang, Dexi Shao, Yijie Zhu, Junjie Wang, and Jian Sun

Electron correlation and electron-lattice interactions are two fundamental aspects of condensed-matter physics, which, combined with nonbound interstitial anionic electrons, can give rise to abundant physical phenomena. In this work, we combined crystal structure prediction with first-principles cal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075122] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

In this work, we combined crystal structure prediction with first-principles cal…

item.title: Pure dephasing increases partition noise in the quantum Hall effect
item.description:
Author(s): C. W. J. Beenakker

Quantum Hall edge channels partition electric charge over $N$ chiral (unidirectional) modes. Intermode scattering leads to partition noise, observed in graphene p-n junctions. While inelastic scattering suppresses this noise by averaging out fluctuations, we show that pure (quasielastic) dephasing m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075416] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): C. W. J. Beenakker

Quantum Hall edge channels partition electric charge over N chiral (unidirectional) modes. Intermode scattering leads to partition noise, observed in graphene p-n junctions. While inelastic scattering suppresses this noise by averaging out fluctuations, we show that pure (quasielastic) dephasing may…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075416] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

item.description:

Crystalline topological insulator nanowires with a magnetic flux threaded through their cross section display Aharanov-Bohm conductance oscillations. A characteristic of these oscillations is the perfectly transmitted mode present at certain values of the magnetic flux, due to the appearance of an e…

${\mathrm{EuAl}}_{4}$, a centrosymmetric magnetic rare-earth transition-metal intermetallic compound, exhibits intriguing properties, such as topological states and topological magnetic textures (skyrmions). The surface termination and morphology of the cleaved surface are still not fully explored, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075418] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

EuAl4, a centrosymmetric magnetic rare-earth transition-metal intermetallic compound, exhibits intriguing properties, such as topological states and topological magnetic textures (skyrmions). The surface termination and morphology of the cleaved surface are still not fully explored, leading to a cha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075418] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026

item.title: Spin-degenerate bulk bands and topological surface states associated with Dirac nodal lines in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Takumi Osumi, Kunihiko Yamauchi, Seigo Souma, Shubhankar Paul, Asuka Honma, Kosuke Nakayama, Kenichi Ozawa, Miho Kitamura, Koji Horiba, Hiroshi Kumigashira, Chiara Bigi, François Bertran, Tamio Oguchi, Takashi Takahashi, Yoshiteru Maeno, and Takafumi Sato

Altermagnets are a novel platform to realize exotic electromagnetic properties distinct from those of conventional ferromagnets and antiferromagnets. We report results of microfocused angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) on ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$, in which its altermagnetic nature has bee…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085116] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Takumi Osumi, Kunihiko Yamauchi, Seigo Souma, Shubhankar Paul, Asuka Honma, Kosuke Nakayama, Kenichi Ozawa, Miho Kitamura, Koji Horiba, Hiroshi Kumigashira, Chiara Bigi, François Bertran, Tamio Oguchi, Takashi Takahashi, Yoshiteru Maeno, and Takafumi Sato

Altermagnets are a novel platform to realize exotic electromagnetic properties distinct from those of conventional ferromagnets and antiferromagnets. We report results of microfocused angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) on RuO2, in which its altermagnetic nature has been under fierce d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085116] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-degenerate bulk bands and topological surface states associated with Dirac nodal lines in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Takumi Osumi, Kunihiko Yamauchi, Seigo Souma, Shubhankar Paul, Asuka Honma, Kosuke Nakayama, Kenichi Ozawa, Miho Kitamura, Koji Horiba, Hiroshi Kumigashira, Chiara Bigi, François Bertran, Tamio Oguchi, Takashi Takahashi, Yoshiteru Maeno, and Takafumi Sato
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Strong-coupling superconductivity near Gross-Neveu quantum criticality in Dirac systems
item.description:
Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Can superconductivity arise without well-defined quasiparticles? This work answers with a striking yes. By studying charge-neutral Dirac fermions near Gross–Neveu quantum criticality, the authors show here that strong critical fluctuations and anomalous fermion dimensions can drive superconductivity, even in the absence of a Fermi surface. Using a strong-coupling framework, inspired by the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model, they identify symmetry-selective pairing channels and demonstrate that well-defined Dirac quasiparticles do not superconduct while ill-defined ones do, demonstrating a unique route for pairing in Dirac materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085119] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Can superconductivity arise without well-defined quasiparticles? This work answers with a striking yes. By studying charge-neutral Dirac fermions near Gross–Neveu quantum criticality, the authors show here that strong critical fluctuations and anomalous fermion dimensions can drive superconductivity, even in the absence of a Fermi surface. Using a strong-coupling framework, inspired by the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model, they identify symmetry-selective pairing channels and demonstrate that well-defined Dirac quasiparticles do not superconduct while ill-defined ones do, demonstrating a unique route for pairing in Dirac materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085119] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Strong-coupling superconductivity near Gross-Neveu quantum criticality in Dirac systems
item.dc:creator: Veronika C. Stangier, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Nonreciprocal spin-wave excitations in Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Aastha Jain and Dheeraj Kumar Singh

We explore the nonreciprocity of spin-wave excitations in the Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnet on a square lattice. Our study reveals that the propagation of spin-wave excitations exhibit nonreciprocal behavior, i.e., spin waves traveling in opposite directions display asymmetry in energy dispersion $ω(\m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054411] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aastha Jain and Dheeraj Kumar Singh

We explore the nonreciprocity of spin-wave excitations in the Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnet on a square lattice. Our study reveals that the propagation of spin-wave excitations exhibit nonreciprocal behavior, i.e., spin waves traveling in opposite directions display asymmetry in energy dispersion ω(q)≠…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054411] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonreciprocal spin-wave excitations in Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnets

item.title: Large anisotropic magnetoresistance in $α$-MnTe induced by strain
item.description:
Author(s): Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin

$α\text{-MnTe}$ is a $p$-type semiconducting altermagnet with a Néel temperature near $300\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\text{K}$. Due to the altermagnetic nature, $\mathcal{PT}$ symmetry is broken and Kramers degeneracy is lifted in the valence band maxima along the $\mathrm{Γ}\text{−}K$ line and the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin

α-MnTe is a p-type semiconducting altermagnet with a Néel temperature near 300K. Due to the altermagnetic nature, PT symmetry is broken and Kramers degeneracy is lifted in the valence band maxima along the Γ−K line and the A point. However, the energy difference is found to be small, and any small s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Large anisotropic magnetoresistance in $α$-MnTe induced by strain
item.dc:creator: Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin

item.title: Two-dimensional flat band on the ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}(011)$ surface: Implication for STM measurements with a superconducting tip
item.description:
Author(s): Jushin Tei, Takeshi Mizushima, and Satoshi Fujimoto

Scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) measurements have been extensively performed on the easily cleavable (011) surface of ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$, using both normal-metal and superconducting tips. Motivated by these experiments, we theoretically investigate the topological surface states on the (011) s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054507] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

Author(s): Jushin Tei, Takeshi Mizushima, and Satoshi Fujimoto

Scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) measurements have been extensively performed on the easily cleavable (011) surface of UTe2, using both normal-metal and superconducting tips. Motivated by these experiments, we theoretically investigate the topological surface states on the (011) surface of UTe2. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054507] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

item.title: Anomalous non-Hermitian topological Anderson insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Mina Ren, Xi Shi, Haitao Jiang, Feng Liu, Hong Chen, and Yong Sun

Strong disorder drives conventional Hermitian systems into Anderson insulating states, suppressing all topological phases. Here, we unveil symmetry-protected, anomalous topological phases in the strong disorder limit of a non-Hermitian system, characterized by a scale-invariant merging of zero-energ…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064202] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mina Ren, Xi Shi, Haitao Jiang, Feng Liu, Hong Chen, and Yong Sun

Strong disorder drives conventional Hermitian systems into Anderson insulating states, suppressing all topological phases. Here, we unveil symmetry-protected, anomalous topological phases in the strong disorder limit of a non-Hermitian system, characterized by a scale-invariant merging of zero-energ…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064202] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

item.title: Tunneling spectroscopy of two-dimensional superconductors with the quantum twisting microscope
item.description:
Author(s): Nemin Wei, Felix von Oppen, and Leonid I. Glazman

The authors develop here a theory for momentum-resolved tunneling spectroscopy of van der Waals superconductors using the quantum twisting microscope and demonstrate how it can directly map the anisotropic superconducting gap in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064502] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nemin Wei, Felix von Oppen, and Leonid I. Glazman

The authors develop here a theory for momentum-resolved tunneling spectroscopy of van der Waals superconductors using the quantum twisting microscope and demonstrate how it can directly map the anisotropic superconducting gap in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064502] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunneling spectroscopy of two-dimensional superconductors with the quantum twisting microscope

item.title: Superfluid stiffness of superconductors with delicate topology
item.description:
Author(s): Tijan Prijon, Sebastian D. Huber, and Kukka-Emilia Huhtinen

We consider superconductivity in two-dimensional delicate topological bands, where the total Chern number vanishes but the Brillouin zone can be divided into subregions with a quantized nontrivial Chern number. We formulate a lower bound on the geometric contribution to the superfluid weight in term…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064504] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

We formulate a lower bound on the geometric contribution to the superfluid weight in term…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064504] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

item.title: Floquet-engineered valley topology with anisotropic response in $1{T}^{′}\text{−}{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ and Janus WSeTe monolayers
item.description:
Author(s): Zhe Li, Haijun Cao, Lijuan Li, Huixia Fu, Mengxue Guan, and Sheng Meng

Valley topology has emerged as a key concept for realizing new classes of quantum states. Here, we investigate Floquet-engineered topological phase transitions in anisotropic $1{T}^{′}\text{−}{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ and its Janus derivative WSeTe monolayers, which exhibit valley-degenerate and valley-po…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075114] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhe Li, Haijun Cao, Lijuan Li, Huixia Fu, Mengxue Guan, and Sheng Meng

Valley topology has emerged as a key concept for realizing new classes of quantum states. Here, we investigate Floquet-engineered topological phase transitions in anisotropic 1T′−WSe2 and its Janus derivative WSeTe monolayers, which exhibit valley-degenerate and valley-polarized characteristics, res…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075114] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

item.title: Quantum oscillations and anisotropic magnetoresistance in the quasi-two-dimensional Dirac nodal line superconductor ${\mathrm{YbSb}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yuxiang Gao, Kevin Allen, Rose Albu Mustaf, Yichen Zhang, Sanu Mishra, Christopher Lane, Marta Zonno, Sergey Gorovikov, Jian-Xin Zhu, Ming Yi, and Emilia Morosan

Recent interest in quantum materials has focused on systems exhibiting both superconductivity and nontrivial band topology as material candidates to realize topological or unconventional superconducting states. So far, superconductivity in most topological materials has been identified as type II. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075115] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuxiang Gao, Kevin Allen, Rose Albu Mustaf, Yichen Zhang, Sanu Mishra, Christopher Lane, Marta Zonno, Sergey Gorovikov, Jian-Xin Zhu, Ming Yi, and Emilia Morosan

Recent interest in quantum materials has focused on systems exhibiting both superconductivity and nontrivial band topology as material candidates to realize topological or unconventional superconducting states. So far, superconductivity in most topological materials has been identified as type II. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075115] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum oscillations and anisotropic magnetoresistance in the quasi-two-dimensional Dirac nodal line superconductor ${\mathrm{YbSb}}_{2}$
